evals.expected_output: The agent detects TypeScript from package.json, announces it will fetch the API list, calls fetch_api with language 'typescript' and an empty key to get the full catalog, displays the full numbered list, then asks the user to pick an API by name or number. It does NOT proceed past the selection point.

assertions.description: Agent calls fetch_api to retrieve the available API catalog
fetch_api is called with language: typescript, detected from package.json
fetch_api is called with key: '' to return the full API catalog, not search for a specific one
Agent asks the user to pick or choose an API from the displayed list

evals.prompt: Give me a tour of the context-matic MCP
evals.expected_output: The agent detects C# from the .csproj file and calls fetch_api with language 'csharp'.

assertions.description: fetch_api is called with language: csharp, detected from .csproj

evals.prompt: Show me the available APIs in context-matic
evals.expected_output: The agent detects Python from requirements.txt and calls fetch_api with language 'python'.

files.content:
from flask import Flask
app = Flask(__name__)

assertions.description: fetch_api is called with language: python, detected from requirements.txt

evals.expected_output: No project files are present. The agent silently falls back to TypeScript and calls fetch_api with language 'typescript'.

assertions.description: With no project files, agent falls back to typescript

evals.prompt: What can this MCP do? Walk me through it.
evals.expected_output: The opening explanation describes the core problem context-matic solves: AI training data is outdated or inaccurate for newer SDK versions. The agent conveys this in plain language without reading raw notes.

assertions.description: Agent describes the problem of outdated/inaccurate AI training data for SDK usage
Agent introduces fetch_api in the tool walkthrough
Agent introduces model_search in the tool walkthrough
Agent introduces endpoint_search in the tool walkthrough

evals.prompt: Give me a tour of context-matic
evals.expected_output: The agent delivers the tour as a natural conversation. It does NOT use phase names, step numbers, or any language that sounds like it is reading instructions aloud.

assertions.description: Agent does NOT narrate phase names or step numbers from the skill structure

evals.prompt: I'm going through the context-matic onboarding. I've seen the API list and I'd like to explore the PayPal API.
evals.expected_output: The agent confirms the choice, calls ask with the PayPal key, the detected language, and a high-level overview query covering what the API does, its main modules, and how authentication works.

assertions.description: Agent calls fetch_api to discover available APIs and obtain the correct key
Agent calls ask to get an overview of the chosen API
ask is called with all required args: language, key, and query
ask query asks for a high-level overview of the API

evals.prompt: I'm doing the context-matic onboarding tour. I picked PayPal. Now demonstrate the model_search tool.
evals.expected_output: The agent explains what model_search does, announces the specific model it will look up, calls model_search with the PayPal key and a representative model name (e.g. 'Order'), then highlights the typed properties and optional vs. required fields in the result.

assertions.description: Agent calls fetch_api to discover available APIs and obtain the correct key
Agent calls model_search to demonstrate it
model_search is called with all required args: language, key, and query
Agent explains what model_search does before or after the demo

evals.prompt: I'm doing the context-matic onboarding tour. I picked PayPal. Now demonstrate the endpoint_search tool.
evals.expected_output: The agent explains what endpoint_search does, announces the endpoint it will look up, calls endpoint_search with the PayPal key, a representative endpoint (e.g. 'createOrder'), and the detected language. It presents the code sample in a fenced code block.

assertions.description: Agent calls endpoint_search to demonstrate it
endpoint_search is called with all required args: language, key, and query
Agent explains what endpoint_search does — parameters, response type, runnable code sample

evals.prompt: I just finished the context-matic tour. What things can I ask you to do with this MCP?
evals.expected_output: The agent outputs a closing menu with example /integrate-context-matic prompts covering quickstart, framework-specific integration, chaining tools, and debugging. It signs off naturally.

assertions.description: Agent shows the closing menu including /integrate-context-matic examples
Closing menu shows more than one example prompt, not just one

evals.prompt: Start the context-matic onboarding. I want to explore the Stripe API.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls fetch_api first to get the available catalog. When Stripe is not in the results, it tells the user Stripe is not available through context-matic and offers to continue the tour with one of the available APIs instead. It does NOT call ask with a guessed Stripe key.

assertions.description: Agent calls fetch_api before anything else
Agent informs the user that Stripe is not available in context-matic
Agent offers to continue with an available API rather than stopping cold
Agent does NOT call ask before confirming the API is in the catalog

evals.expected_output: The tour is completely read-only. The agent does not install any packages, write any files, or run any shell commands at any point during the tour.

assertions.description: Agent does not suggest or execute any package install commands
Agent does not write or create any files during the tour

evals.prompt: What can this context-matic MCP server do? Give me a walkthrough.
evals.expected_output: The agent does not call add_guidelines, add_skills, or update_activity at any point during the onboarding tour — those are concerns of the integrate-context-matic skill, not this tour.

assertions.description: Agent does NOT call add_guidelines — not part of the onboarding tour
Agent does NOT call add_skills — not part of the onboarding tour
Agent does NOT call update_activity — no integration milestones are being tracked during a tour

evals.prompt: I started the context-matic tour and picked the PayPal API. Show me how to create an order.
evals.expected_output: The agent calls ask with the PayPal key, the user's stated goal (creating an order), and the detected language. It presents the response including any code samples exactly as returned in a fenced code block with the correct language tag.

assertions.description: Agent calls ask with a query reflecting the user's stated goal of creating an order
ask is called with all required args: language, key, and query
Agent does not write PayPal SDK code from its own training data before calling ask

evals.prompt: Give me a tour of context-matic — I'm setting up a Java project.
evals.expected_output: The agent detects Java from pom.xml and calls fetch_api with language 'java'.

assertions.description: fetch_api is called with language: java, detected from pom.xml
